sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Nasdaq gives NetClass 180 days to restore $1 share price
feed.summary: NetClass Technology Inc. (NTCL) has received a notice from Nasdaq stating that its Class A ordinary shares have fallen below the required $1 minimum bid price for 30 consecutive business days. The company has been granted a 180-calendar-day compliance period, until July 27, 2026, to regain compliance. While evaluating options, including a potential reverse stock split, NetClass Technology acknowledged there's no guarantee it will successfully meet the requirement.

feed.title: Rollins Inc chief admin officer Tesh sells $20k in stock
feed.summary: Rollins Inc.'s Chief Administrative Officer, Tesh Thomas D., sold 323 shares of company stock worth $20,294 on January 28, 2026, to cover tax withholding obligations. The pest control company, valued over $30 billion, has a strong dividend history and high gross profit margins, though InvestingPro analysis suggests it may be overvalued. Analysts have mixed views on the stock, with some raising price targets while others maintain a neutral or hold rating ahead of its earnings report on February 11, 2026.

feed.title: Qiagen Reports Goldman Sachs Builds 3.15% Voting Stake via Shares and Derivatives
feed.summary: Qiagen announced that Goldman Sachs Group Inc. has amassed an aggregate 3.15% interest in its capital and voting rights through various subsidiaries and financial instruments. This significant institutional investment, disclosed via the Dutch financial markets regulator AFM, includes ordinary shares, swaps, options, warrants, and convertible bonds. The presence of Goldman Sachs as a major shareholder could influence market perception and trading liquidity for Qiagen (QGEN).

feed.title: What Lies Beneath: A Holdings-Level View on Private Equity
feed.summary: This article discusses the importance of holdings-level indexes in private equity analysis, which complement traditional fund-level benchmarks by providing a more granular view of underlying company performance, decoupled from fund-specific economics like fees and leverage. The authors argue that this approach offers better attribution across sectors, regions, and strategies, highlighting distinct performance drivers in buyout and venture capital, particularly in APAC IT. By combining both fund- and holdings-level views, investors can gain a more comprehensive understanding of private equity performance and risk.

feed.title: Barclays Discloses 3.26% Voting Stake in Qiagen
feed.summary: Barclays Plc has disclosed a 3.26% voting stake in Qiagen, as reported by the Dutch Authority for the Financial Markets (AFM). This aggregate position includes both real and potential interests in ordinary shares, derivatives, and other financial instruments. This signals continued institutional investor interest in Qiagen.

feed.title: Freeport-McMoRan Inc. stock outperforms competitors despite losses on the day
feed.summary: Freeport-McMoRan Inc. (FCX) stock fell 1.63% on Friday but still managed to outperform its competitors, including BHP Group Ltd. and Rio Tinto PLC, which experienced larger losses. The stock traded between $40.59 and $41.35, closing at $40.69, while the S&P 500 also saw a slight decline. The article highlights FCX's stronger performance relative to its peers despite the market downturn.

feed.title: Regeneron (REGN) Reports Strong Q4 2025 Revenue Growth
feed.summary: Regeneron Pharmaceuticals reported a strong 3% year-over-year increase in total revenue for Q4 2025, driven by significant sales growth in key products like DUPIXENT, LIBTAYO, and EYLEA HD. The company is preparing for 18 new Phase III studies and anticipates at least four FDA approvals in 2026, including three new molecular entities, positioning it for continued growth in ophthalmology and immunology. Despite a prior 3-year revenue decline, Regeneron maintains strong profitability and a robust balance sheet, though insider selling activity is noted.

feed.title: Beyond Air (XAIR) Announces Sale of 7.86 Million Shares of Commo
feed.summary: Beyond Air (XAIR) is selling approximately 7.86 million shares of common stock to raise capital, despite facing significant financial challenges including negative margins and a distressed Altman Z-Score. The company, which specializes in medical devices and biopharmaceuticals, operates in a highly competitive sector and exhibits high stock volatility. This capital raise is intended to optimize financial resources, but substantial underlying financial issues persist.

feed.title: Blue Foundry Shareholders Approve Merger with Fulton Financial
feed.summary: Blue Foundry Bancorp shareholders have approved a merger agreement with Fulton Financial Corporation, making Fulton the surviving entity. The merger received substantial majority support from represented shares, clearing a key hurdle for the transaction. This move signals investor confidence in combining Blue Foundry with a larger banking group, potentially reshaping its future operations and competitive stance in regional banking.

feed.title: Eastman looks to recycling plant to drive growth
feed.summary: Eastman is focusing on its chemical recycling plant in Kingsport to drive growth in 2026, with expectations for accelerated revenue from its Renew resin line, especially Tritan Renew. Despite a challenging economy affecting consumer demand and slower ramp-up from some customers, Eastman anticipates a significant increase in packaging revenue, partly due to partnerships with brands like PepsiCo. The company is also re-evaluating plans for a second recycling plant to ensure capital efficiency and better return on investment.

feed.title: SmartStop Self Storage REIT Declares February 2026 Dividend
feed.summary: SmartStop Self Storage REIT (SMA) announced a February 2026 monthly dividend of $0.12273973 per share, payable on March 13, 2026, which translates to an annualized rate of $1.60. TipRanks' AI Analyst, Spark, rates SMA as Neutral, citing strong free cash flow and strategic growth but also weak price momentum and ongoing profitability challenges. The REIT focuses on owning and operating self-storage properties.

feed.title: Marathon Petroleum (MPC) Declares $1.00 Quarterly Dividend; 2.3% Yield
feed.summary: Marathon Petroleum (MPC) has declared a quarterly dividend of $1.00 per share, which annualizes to $4 and represents a 2.3% yield. The dividend is payable on March 10, 2026, to shareholders of record as of February 18, 2026, with an ex-dividend date of February 17, 2026.

feed.title: Certain BlackRock Closed-End Funds Announce Estimated Sources of Distributions
feed.summary: BlackRock announced the estimated sources of distributions for several of its closed-end funds, paid on January 30, 2026. The distributions comprise net income, net realized short-term gains, net realized long-term gains, and return of capital. The company clarified that these allocations are estimates and the actual tax reporting will depend on the funds' performance throughout the fiscal year.

feed.title: Marathon Petroleum will pay $1.00 a share to investors in March
feed.summary: Marathon Petroleum (NYSE: MPC) has announced a quarterly dividend of $1.00 per common share. The dividend will be payable on March 10, 2026, to shareholders of record as of the close of business on February 18, 2026. This decision was made by the company's board of directors, highlighted in a press release from Findlay, Ohio.

feed.title: Red Wolf weapon takes Marine strikes 200 nautical miles out
feed.summary: L3Harris Technologies has been selected by the U.S. Naval Air Systems Command for the Marine Corps’ Precision Attack Strike Munition program, developing their Red Wolf vehicle. This system will provide long-range precision weapons capabilities, extending the strike range of vertical take-off and landing platforms to 200 nautical miles, significantly enhancing the Marines' arsenal. The Red Wolf aims to offer a cost-effective alternative to expensive munitions, addressing critical military needs highlighted by recent conflicts.

feed.title: Kroger's Board of Directors Declares Quarterly Dividend
feed.summary: The Kroger Co.'s Board of Directors has declared a quarterly dividend of 35 cents per share, payable on March 1, 2026, to shareholders as of February 13, 2026. The company highlights its capital allocation strategy, which includes investing in the business, maintaining an investment-grade debt rating, and returning capital to shareholders, with a history of increasing dividends since 2006.

feed.title: KKR Real Estate Finance Trust Inc. Tax Treatment of 2025 Dividends
feed.summary: KKR Real Estate Finance Trust Inc. (KREF) announced the tax treatment for its 2025 common stock and Series A Cumulative Redeemable Preferred Stock dividends. The press release includes detailed tables summarizing the distribution of cash, ordinary dividends, qualified dividends, capital gain dividends, return of capital, and Section 199A dividends for both common and preferred stock throughout the 2025 tax year. KREF is a real estate finance company specializing in originating and acquiring senior commercial real estate loans, managed by an affiliate of KKR & Co. Inc.

feed.title: Adamas Trust Announces Tax Treatment of 2025 Dividend Distributions
feed.summary: Adamas Trust, Inc. announced the tax treatment of its common and preferred stock dividends paid in 2025 to assist stockholders with their tax reporting requirements. The company provided detailed classifications for various dividend types, including ordinary, qualified, capital gain, and non-dividend distributions, for its common stock and several series of preferred stock. Stockholders are advised to consult their personal tax advisors for specific guidance, as Adamas does not provide tax advice.

feed.title: Adamas Trust spells out how 2025 dividends show up on 1099s
feed.summary: Adamas Trust (ADAM) has issued a detailed tax classification for its 2025 dividend distributions across common and multiple preferred share series to help stockholders with their Form 1099 reporting. The announcement specifies per-share amounts for ordinary, qualified, and capital gain dividends, alongside clarifying the tax treatment for a January 2026 payment under IRC §857(b)(9). The company also confirmed that no portion of its 2025 dividends is expected to be considered excess inclusion income.

feed.title: Conagra’s Frozen Food Trends Spark Market Interest Amid Price Target Downgrade
feed.summary: Conagra Brands (CAG) stock has seen a 3.7% increase, driven by investor optimism despite a recent price target downgrade by Wells Fargo from $19 to $18. The company is strategically focused on four key themes in the booming $93.5 billion U.S. frozen food market: protein dishes, restaurant-inspired meals, family-sized options, and all-day breakfast. While Conagra faces financial challenges like declining revenue and significant debt, its strong operational cash flows and market positioning in frozen foods suggest potential for future growth, tempered by the need for strong operational execution.

feed.title: Raymond James Lands $1B, Four-Advisor Team from Merrill
feed.summary: Raymond James announced it has recruited a four-advisor team from Bank of America’s Merrill Lynch, which previously managed $1 billion in client assets. The team, operating as Thrift Private Wealth of Raymond James in Easton, Md., includes advisors Garrett Thrift, Kara Burt, Blake Saulsbury, and Wade Oursler. This move comes as Raymond James reported strong recruiting results for its fiscal first quarter, while Bank of America experienced advisor attrition.

feed.title: ASGN Inc: Quiet Rally Or The Calm Before A Turn in Tech Staffing?
feed.summary: ASGN Inc. has seen a steady uptrend, trading near its 52-week highs, leading investors to question whether this indicates market fatigue or the early stages of a re-evaluation for the IT services company. The stock's performance over the past year, while not explosive, shows a respectable gain, outperforming many cyclical names despite macro headwinds. Wall Street analysts are cautiously optimistic, maintaining mostly "Buy" or "Overweight" ratings, focusing on ASGN's diversified business model and consistent cash generation.

feed.title: Fund Update: New $8.7M $RTX stock position opened by CITIZENS FINANCIAL GROUP INC|RI
feed.summary: CITIZENS FINANCIAL GROUP INC/RI has opened a new $8.7 million position in RTX, as revealed in a recent SEC 13F filing for the report period ending 12-31-2025. This article also details significant movements by other institutional investors, insider trading activities, government contracts secured by RTX, and congressional stock trading involving RTX shares. Analyst ratings and price targets are also provided, indicating generally positive sentiment towards the stock.

feed.title: Air Products & Chemicals, Inc. SEC 10-Q Report
feed.summary: Air Products & Chemicals, Inc. has released its Q3 fiscal year 10-Q report, showcasing robust financial performance including increased sales, operating income, and net income. Key highlights include sales of $3,102.5 million, strong earnings per share, and significant progress on the NEOM Green Hydrogen Project in Saudi Arabia. The company is actively focusing on clean energy initiatives and has implemented a global cost reduction plan alongside managing project exit costs.

feed.title: Seagate Technology Holdings plc SEC 10-Q Report
feed.summary: Seagate Technology Holdings plc has released its Form 10-Q report for the December 2025 quarter, showing strong financial performance with $2,825 million in revenue and $593 million in net income. The company attributed this success to robust demand in data center markets and effective cost management. Seagate plans to continue focusing on data center and Edge IoT markets, anticipating sustained growth driven by AI-related demand and investments in data center infrastructure.

feed.title: The Truth About DuPont de Nemours: Is This ‘Boring’ Stock Actually a Secret Weapon?
feed.summary: DuPont de Nemours is portrayed as a stable, "boring" stock with real products and underrated potential, appealing to long-term investors interested in industrials, dividends, and materials for EVs and semiconductors. Despite lacking viral hype, the company is quietly growing by stacking cash and reinventing itself, making it a potentially valuable asset for a diversified portfolio. Its focus on essential materials and contracts offers slower but more stable growth compared to volatile meme stocks, even with risks like cyclical demand and legacy legal issues.

feed.title: DOV Q4 Deep Dive: Margin Expansion and Order Growth Shape Outlook Amid Cautious Guidance
feed.summary: Dover (NYSE:DOV) exceeded Q4 CY2025 revenue and adjusted EPS expectations, with sales up 8.8% year-on-year. Despite strong bookings in growth markets and operational improvements, the market reacted negatively due to cautious 2026 guidance, reflecting concerns over rising input costs and macroeconomic uncertainties. Management remains focused on volume leverage, past restructuring benefits, and synergy extraction from acquisitions to achieve long-term growth.

feed.title: Alexandria Real Estate Equities (NYSE:ARE) Price Target Raised to $57.00
feed.summary: Jefferies Financial Group raised its price target for Alexandria Real Estate Equities (NYSE:ARE) to $57.00 from $47.00, maintaining a "hold" rating, despite a consensus "Hold" and an average target of $65.86 from analysts. The real estate investment trust reported a narrow beat on Q4 estimates and announced a $500 million share buyback and FY2026 EPS guidance, but it also faced a large loss and ongoing leasing challenges in the life-sciences market. Insider transactions include a director's significant share purchase, while institutional investors have adjusted their positions in the company.

feed.title: Stocks making big moves this week: Lockheed Martin, Texas Instruments, Western Digital, Humana, and Brown & Brown
feed.summary: This article highlights several stocks that experienced significant movement this week. Lockheed Martin and Texas Instruments saw gains due to strong earnings and optimistic forecasts, respectively. Western Digital also rose after an analyst's price target increase. Conversely, Humana and Brown & Brown both fell after Humana faced a smaller-than-expected Medicare Advantage payment rate increase and Brown & Brown reported mixed fourth-quarter results with a revenue shortfall.

feed.title: UDR, Inc. Announces Dates for Fourth Quarter and Full-Year 2025 Earnings Release, Webcast, and Conference Call
feed.summary: UDR, Inc. announced it will release its fourth quarter and full-year 2025 financial results on Monday, February 9, 2026, after market close. A public webcast and conference call will follow on Tuesday, February 10, 2026, at 12:00 p.m. Eastern Time, during which company officers will discuss results and recent events. Financial analysts will have a Q&A session, while other participants can listen only.

feed.title: Better Long-Term Buy: This Emerging Player or the Industry Leader?
feed.summary: This article compares AbbVie (ABBV), an established pharmaceutical giant with a strong immunology portfolio and reliable dividends, to Abivax (ABVX), a clinical-stage biotech with a promising ulcerative colitis treatment still in development. AbbVie offers stability and income for risk-averse investors, while Abivax presents massive upside potential along with significant risks due to its early stage. The choice depends on an investor's risk tolerance and investment goals.

feed.title: KKR Real Estate Finance Trust Inc. Declares Preferred Stock Dividend
feed.summary: KKR Real Estate Finance Trust Inc. (KREF) announced that its Board of Directors has declared a dividend of $0.40625 per share for its 6.50% Series A Cumulative Redeemable Preferred Stock. This dividend represents an annual payment of $1.625 per share and is payable on March 13, 2026, to stockholders of record as of February 27, 2026. KREF is a real estate finance company focusing on originating and acquiring senior loans secured by commercial real estate properties.

feed.title: UDR, Inc. Announces Dates for Fourth Quarter and Full-Year 2025 Earnings Release, Webcast, and Conference Call
feed.summary: UDR, Inc. (NYSE: UDR) announced it will release its fourth quarter and full-year 2025 financial results on Monday, February 9, 2026, after market close. A webcast and conference call for the results will be held on Tuesday, February 10, 2026, at 12:00 p.m. Eastern Time. During this call, company officers will review results, discuss recent events, and conduct a Q&A session for registered financial analysts.

feed.title: AMH Announces Tax Treatment of 2025 Distributions
feed.summary: AMH (NYSE: AMH) has announced the tax treatment for its 2025 cash distributions, categorizing them into Ordinary Dividend Income, Qualified Dividend Income, and Capital Gain Distributions for common, Series G, and Series H preferred shares. The company provided a detailed breakdown of these classifications for each quarterly distribution. Shareholders are advised to consult their tax advisors regarding the specific tax treatment, as the company's 2025 tax return has not yet been filed.

feed.title: Phillips 66 Borger, Texas Refinery Reports Process Upset
feed.summary: Phillips 66's refinery in Borger, Texas, has reported a process upset. This information was disseminated by Refinitiv and Reuters, affecting market data and potentially contributing to ESG and regulation discussions for PSX.

feed.title: Arbor Realty Trust Announces Tax Treatment of 2025 Dividends
feed.summary: Arbor Realty Trust, Inc. has announced the tax treatment for its 2025 dividend distributions for both common and preferred shares. For tax reporting, 100% of these distributions will be classified as dividend income. The announcement provides detailed summaries, including non-qualified and qualified dividend amounts, for common shares (ABR) and several series of preferred stock (ABR-PD, ABR-PE, ABR-PF), clarifying that no excess inclusion income will be passed through to shareholders for 2025.

feed.title: Kroger closing all 18 Little Clinics in GA, including Macon, Warner Robins
feed.summary: Kroger announced the closure of all 18 of its The Little Clinic locations in Georgia, effective April 4th, 2026. This includes two clinics in Middle Georgia: one in Warner Robins and one in Macon. Kroger encouraged patients to continue using their in-store pharmacies for healthcare needs, but did not provide a specific reason for the closures.

feed.title: Intuit Signs Credit Agreement With JPMorgan Chase Bank and Other Lenders
feed.summary: Intuit has entered into a new $5.8 billion unsecured short-term revolving credit facility with JPMorgan Chase Bank and other lenders. This facility, maturing on March 31, 2026, is intended to provide liquidity for Intuit's early tax refund offering. As of the signing date, Intuit has not drawn on this facility.

feed.title: BranchOut Food Signs Senior Secured Promissory Note With Kaufman Kapital
feed.summary: BranchOut Food (BOF) has signed a Senior Secured Promissory Note for $1.5 million with Kaufman Kapital to boost its liquidity. The one-year note includes an 8% annual interest rate and is secured by most of the company's assets. The proceeds are intended to support working capital and general corporate purposes.

feed.title: U.S. Markets Closed Down Friday; Unity Software Posted Biggest Loss
feed.summary: U.S. stock indexes closed down on Friday, with the Dow Jones, S&P 500, and Nasdaq Composite all experiencing declines. Unity Software posted the biggest loss among the listed stocks. This marks a negative end to the trading week for the U.S. markets.

feed.title: 12 Information Technology Stocks Moving In Friday's After-Market Session
feed.summary: This article lists 12 information technology stocks that experienced significant movement during Friday's after-market trading session. It details the gainers, including TechCreate Group, AmpliTech Group, X3 Holdings, UTime, Rubrik, and Wetouch Technology, along with their respective percentage increases and market capitalizations. The article also identifies the losers, such as Brand Engagement Network, XIAO-I, BIO-key International, Wellchange Holdings, HIVE Digital Technologies, and MaxLinear, noting their percentage decreases and market values.

feed.title: Argus Raises PT on Sanmina (SANM) Stock
feed.summary: Argus has raised its price target on Sanmina (SANM) stock to $200 from $170 while maintaining a "Buy" rating. This decision follows Sanmina's Q1 2026 earnings beat and its strong manufacturing presence in the Americas. The company's agile manufacturing infrastructure and global footprint are expected to mitigate impacts from tariffs.

feed.title: Needham Maintains Buy Rating on TeraWulf (WULF) Stock, Maintains PT
feed.summary: Needham analyst John Todaro has maintained a "Buy" rating and a $21.00 price target for TeraWulf Inc. (WULF). The analysis highlights TeraWulf's transition from a traditional miner to a high-performance computing and AI infrastructure provider. This positive outlook is supported by WULF's significant IT load under contract, plans for capacity expansion, and a strategic approach to site selection that minimizes development time and risk.

feed.title: DOL warns pro-plaintiff ruling in Honeywell case could threaten 401(k) matches
feed.summary: The Department of Labor (DOL) has issued a warning that a pro-plaintiff ruling in the Honeywell case could jeopardize 401(k) matches for employees. The article highlights this significant concern regarding retirement benefits. Further details about the case and its potential implications are presented.

feed.title: How a TD Bank teller laundered millions for $50 bribes
feed.summary: A former TD Bank employee, Leonardo Ayala, pleaded guilty to charges related to a multi-million-dollar money laundering scheme in Colombia, revealing specific methods used to bypass internal controls. Ayala, along with co-conspirators, used remote servicing capabilities and teller authority to issue numerous debit cards for shell company accounts, receiving bribes for these services. This case sheds light on the granular compliance failures contributing to TD Bank's historic $3 billion penalty and ongoing remediation efforts to fix its anti-money-laundering programs.

feed.title: This week in insurance: Prudential boosts Malaysia stake, Ping An tops brand rankings, new partnerships emerge
feed.summary: In the past week, the insurance sector in Asia saw significant activity with Prudential increasing its stake in a Malaysian holding company to 70%, and Ping An Insurance being recognized as China's most valuable insurance brand for the tenth consecutive year. New partnerships and product launches also occurred, including Allianz Indonesia's collaboration with PT Allianz Global Investors Asset Management Indonesia and Great Eastern's debut of a physical gold investment-linked policy in Singapore. QBE Automotive Protection also expanded its reach through a partnership with Malayan Insurance in the Philippines to offer extended warranty coverage.

feed.title: Tompkins: Fourth Quarter Earnings Overview
feed.summary: Tompkins Financial Corp. announced strong financial results for its fourth quarter, reporting a net profit of $96.2 million and earnings of $6.70 per share. After excluding one-time gains, adjusted earnings were $1.78 per share, with total revenue reaching $226.7 million and net revenue exceeding analysts' expectations at $194.8 million. For the full year, the company achieved a total profit of $161.1 million ($11.24 per share) and annual revenue of $446.6 million.

feed.title: Ringing In Your Ears? Texas Roadhouse Wants to Help Silence Tinnitus
feed.summary: Texas Roadhouse is partnering with the American Tinnitus Association to raise funds for tinnitus research. On February 2nd, the restaurant chain will donate 100% of its profits to the organization in honor of its late founder, Kent Taylor, who suffered from the condition. This "Dine-to-Donate" event kicks off Tinnitus Awareness Week and aims to support efforts for a cure or treatment for the frustrating condition affecting millions.

feed.title: Court Halts Construction at Royal Caribbean’s Perfect Day Mexico Cruise Port
feed.summary: Construction at Royal Caribbean's Perfect Day Mexico cruise port has been temporarily halted due to a legal challenge from a local environmental group, DMAS, which secured an injunction. DMAS claims that the change of land use for over 107 hectares was illegally reconfigured and that public consultation should have occurred, despite municipal leaders stating it was not mandatory for this specific amendment. A court hearing is expected soon to address the injunction, while Royal Caribbean maintains they will not impact protected mangrove areas.

feed.title: F5, Inc. (FFIV) Shareholders Who Lost Money Have Opportunity to Lead Securities Fraud Lawsuit
feed.summary: The Law Offices of Howard G. Smith announced a securities fraud class action lawsuit against F5, Inc. (FFIV) on behalf of investors who suffered losses. The lawsuit alleges that F5 failed to disclose a significant security incident between October 28, 2024, and October 27, 2025, which would have put its clientele's security and the company's future prospects at risk. Investors who lost money are encouraged to contact the law firm before the lead plaintiff deadline of February 17, 2026.
